extra.message: User input from params['id'] is directly concatenated into SQL query. Attacker can inject SQL syntax to bypass authentication, extract data, or modify database. No parameterized queries used.
User input from params['v'] is directly substituted into HTML content via regex replacement without HTML encoding. Attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (Cross-site Scripting)

extra.message: User input from params['object'] is deserialized using pickle.loads() without validation. Pickle can execute arbitrary code during deserialization, leading to remote code execution.
User input from params['path'] is passed directly to open() after abspath(). Attacker can use ../ sequences to read arbitrary files from the filesystem, bypassing intended directory restrictions.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory

extra.message: User input from params['domain'] is passed to subprocess.run() with shell=True. Attacker can inject shell metacharacters to execute arbitrary OS commands.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command

extra.message: XML parsing with lxml.etree.XMLParser(load_dtd=True, resolve_entities=True, no_network=False). User input from params['xml'] is parsed with DTD loading and entity resolution enabled, allowing XXE attacks to read files or cause DoS.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message: User input from params['name'] is directly concatenated into XPath expression. Attacker can break out of the XPath context and query arbitrary nodes, leaking sensitive data.

cwe: CWE-643: Improper Neutralization of Data into an XPath Expression

extra.message: User input from params['comment'] is directly concatenated into SQL INSERT statement using string formatting. Attacker can inject SQL syntax to modify database or execute arbitrary queries.
User input from params['include'] is read from disk or URL and executed using exec() with arbitrary environment variables. This allows remote code execution with full server privileges.

cwe: CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code

extra.message: User input from params['include'] is passed to open() for local file access. Attacker can use path traversal sequences to read arbitrary files before execution.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory

extra.message: User input from params['redir'] is inserted directly into meta refresh redirect without validation. Attacker can redirect users to malicious sites for phishing or malware distribution.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site

extra.message: JSONP callback parameter from params['callback'] is concatenated directly into response without sanitization. Attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript that executes in the context of the user's browser.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (Cross-site Scripting)

extra.message: While username is sanitized with regex, the password parameter is concatenated directly into SQL query without any protection. Attacker can inject SQL using the password field to bypass authentication or extract data.
